title: Mental illness stigma as a moderator in the relationship between religiosity and help-seeking attitudes among Muslims from 16 Arab countries
book: Mental illness stigma as a moderator in the relationship between religiosity and help-seeking attitudes among Muslims from 16 Arab countries

node_types.description: Stores information about the degree and nature of religious beliefs and practices, as measured by standardized scales.

properties.name: religiosity_score
properties.description: Quantitative measure of religiosity (e.g., ARS score)

properties.name: religious_beliefs
properties.description: Qualitative description of religious beliefs relevant to mental health

properties.name: religious_practices
properties.description: Practices or rituals associated with religiosity

supported_by_chapters: Mental illness stigma as a moderator in the relationship between religiosity and help-seeking attitudes among Muslims from 16 Arab countries

node_types.description: Stores information about attitudes, beliefs, and stigma related to mental illness, as measured by standardized scales.

properties.name: stigma_score
properties.description: Quantitative measure of stigma (e.g., CAMI score)

properties.name: stigma_sources
properties.description: Cultural or religious sources of stigma (e.g., supernatural attributions)

properties.name: stigma_type
properties.description: Type of stigma (e.g., public, self-stigma, perceived)

supported_by_chapters: Mental illness stigma as a moderator in the relationship between religiosity and help-seeking attitudes among Muslims from 16 Arab countries

node_types.description: Stores information about attitudes toward seeking professional psychological help.

properties.name: help_seeking_score
properties.description: Quantitative measure of help-seeking attitude (e.g., ATSPPH-SF score)

properties.name: preferred_help_source
properties.description: Preferred source of help (e.g., professional, religious healer, traditional healer)

properties.name: barriers_to_help_seeking
properties.description: Identified barriers to seeking professional help

supported_by_chapters: Mental illness stigma as a moderator in the relationship between religiosity and help-seeking attitudes among Muslims from 16 Arab countries

node_types.description: Stores information about cultural or religious explanations for mental illness.

properties.name: attribution_type
properties.description: Type of attribution (e.g., punishment from God, Jinn, Evil eye)

properties.name: attribution_prevalence
properties.description: Prevalence or frequency of this attribution in the population

supported_by_chapters: Mental illness stigma as a moderator in the relationship between religiosity and help-seeking attitudes among Muslims from 16 Arab countries

node_types.description: Stores information about interventions designed to reduce mental illness stigma in religious or cultural contexts.

properties.name: intervention_type
properties.description: Type of intervention (e.g., educational, community-based, religious leader involvement)

properties.name: cultural_sensitivity
properties.description: Degree to which the intervention is tailored to Islamic or Arab cultural context

properties.name: effectiveness
properties.description: Reported effectiveness or outcomes of the intervention

supported_by_chapters: Mental illness stigma as a moderator in the relationship between religiosity and help-seeking attitudes among Muslims from 16 Arab countries

edge_types.description: Indicates that religiosity influences attitudes toward seeking professional psychological help.

properties.name: moderated_by_stigma
properties.description: Whether and how the influence is moderated by mental illness stigma

supported_by_chapters: Mental illness stigma as a moderator in the relationship between religiosity and help-seeking attitudes among Muslims from 16 Arab countries

edge_types.description: Indicates that mental illness stigma moderates the relationship between religiosity and help-seeking attitudes.

properties.name: moderation_effect
properties.description: Description or measure of the moderation effect

supported_by_chapters: Mental illness stigma as a moderator in the relationship between religiosity and help-seeking attitudes among Muslims from 16 Arab countries

edge_types.description: Indicates that certain cultural or religious attributions contribute to mental illness stigma.

properties.name: contribution_strength
properties.description: Strength or significance of the contribution

supported_by_chapters: Mental illness stigma as a moderator in the relationship between religiosity and help-seeking attitudes among Muslims from 16 Arab countries

edge_types.description: Indicates that an anti-stigma intervention is designed to reduce mental illness stigma.
